main.description: A dictionary entry of the Leksis dictionary. Records prove authorship, not ownership: any user may publish an entry record, and a record carrying a `subject` reference is a proposed new version of the entry that record belongs to — the AppView treats the latest version as current and archives previous ones. The record is the source of truth for its content; the AppView indexes only what search needs.

languageID.description: Well-formed IETF BCP 47 tag of the entry's language, normalized to lowercase (e.g. "br", "br-gw").

orthography.description: Valid spellings of the word: variants, archaic forms, regional spellings. The first item is the canonical form.

transcription.description: Phonetic transcription of the word in the International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA), e.g. "[ˈbrɛːzɔ̃nɛk]". Record-only content, not indexed by the AppView.

categories.description: Ordered grammatical categories of the entry (e.g. "n."/"noun", "f."/"feminine"). Freeform, not an enforced vocabulary.

otherForms.description: Other grammatical forms of the word (plural, gerund, construct state…), each an abbreviation-tagged form (e.g. "pl."/"plural" + "bugale"). The abbreviations are drawn from the same per-language pool as the entry's categories and definition notes; the AppView indexes each form's spelling for search so an inflected form leads back to its entry.

definitions.description: The entry's definitions as a flat list, each item a node of a hierarchy of up to three dimensions addressed by its `place`. A node whose place ends in a non-zero index is a leaf (the definition proper: it carries `text`); a node whose place ends in 0 is a group node (a heading that carries notes but no text — e.g. the "transitive" grouping over several senses). Bare grouping (a group with no notes) is left implicit — it need not appear as its own item; the hierarchy is inferred from the leaves. The array is sorted by `place` in reading order.

notes.description: Entry-level free-text notes shown below the definitions (usage remarks, provenance, editorial comments). Freeform prose, not abbreviations.

references.description: Bibliographic references and sources for the entry, shown at the bottom of the entry.

subject.description: AT URI of the entry record version this record modifies. Absent for a brand-new entry; present on a proposed modification, so the AppView can attach this version to the existing entry.

todo.description: Pending-work notes: each item is one task this version still needs (e.g. an ingestion bot flagging one unverified aspect), so several bots or editors can each track their own item. Empty or absent means nothing is pending. The AppView indexes only whether any item exists, as a boolean flag.

deleted.description: Marks this version as a deletion: the entry is withdrawn from search under this record, but stays reachable at its entryKey for legacy links and to contest the deletion. Requires `deletionReason`. `orthography`/`categories`/`definitions` still carry the version's last known content (or may repeat the prior version's), since the lexicon requires them, but the AppView excludes a deleted version's orthography from the search index.

deletionReason.description: Required when `deleted` is true: why this entry was withdrawn (e.g. "duplicate of br-gwerzenn-1b76", "not an attested word").

redirectTo.description: When `deleted` is true and the reason is a duplicate, the entryKey of the correct entry to redirect readers to.

createdAt.description: Client-declared creation time of this record version.

long.description: Full form (e.g. "noun", "botany") — the only required half: a lone form is always the full one, displayed directly with nothing revealed on hover.

short.description: Optional abbreviated display form (e.g. "n.", "bot."); when present it is shown instead of the full form, which appears on hover.

place.description: Address of the node in the entry's tree: one index per dimension, deepest last. The LAST index is the node type — non-zero means a leaf (the definition proper, so `text` is required), 0 means a group node (a heading carrying notes but no text). A non-last index of 0 means "no grouping at that dimension", so a place can render shallower than its length (e.g. [0,1,1] = I. 1., [1] = [0,1] = [0,0,1] = 1.). In display each non-zero index n shows as the n-th label of its dimension and each 0 is skipped; numbering by displayed depth: 1 → arabic; 2 → roman, arabic; 3 → letters, roman, arabic (so [1,2,0] = A. II., [1,1,1] = A. I. 1.). Across the entry, places are sorted in reading order and sibling indices are contiguous from 1.

notes.description: Ordered abbreviation notes shown before the node's content (e.g. "by ext."/"by extension", "arch."/"archaic"). Allowed on leaves and group nodes alike. May be empty.

plainNotes.description: Free-text notes shown before the node's content — remarks that are neither abbreviations nor part of the definition text. Allowed on leaves and group nodes alike.

text.description: The definition text. Required on and only on a leaf (place ending in a non-zero index); a group node (place ending in 0) must not carry text.

annotation.description: The abbreviation labelling this form (e.g. "pl."/"plural"), from the same per-language pool as categories and notes.

form.description: The spelling of the inflected form (e.g. "bugale").

text.description: The reference's display text (a citation, source name or note).

url.description: Optional URL the reference links to.
